comment.body:
The EU is lead by Germany, the Chinese are eating Germany’s lunch, as much as they possibly can. So there is no natural alliance. Now it’s true Russia’s behaviour and Trumps USA has forced Europe to consider paying for it own defence. 

Trump wants good relationships with Russia because it means if USA has to fight China Russia won’t be an ally of China. This is naivety on a Chamberlain like level. Russia is Italy as China is to Germany in 1938. Putin will never trust USA as it changes its mind every 4yrs. If a war starts Putin will probably wait to see who’s winning and jump on board. Just like Italy did in 1940. 

Trump seems to see a multi polar world with three great powers USA,China and Russia. This is bollocks, Russia is a busted flush with economy similar in size to Spain and a technological backwater. The third great power is Europe. The USA alliance’s of NATO, Japan,Korea, Australia, New Zealand, Taiwan is around 62% of world GDP. A formidable foe indeed. It would be really stupid to break that up for an sort of lose understanding  with Russia.

The USA is the world reserve currency so that gives them the privilege of sending money abroad in return for stuff (run huge deficit) because people are desperate for the $’s. The flip side of that is you lose your capacity to produce as it moves overseas, your capacity to produce is what matters in any kinetic war. Free trade has allowed Wall St to get rich as Americas working class have been impoverished, this is what the US has belatedly realised. They have weakened their security and impoverished their people in the name of globalisation ( but really to enrich certain people).

Trumps team have realised this is unsustainable and dangerous so they want to weaken the $ And  impose reciprocal tariffs, because the truth is europe, Canada etc have been taking the piss. Already it appears Trump is in talks with UK about special economic zone (free trade deal) and I expect he will be with others. He doesn’t like the EU and I think he is right everything they have done since the single market has made the place less democratic and weaker economically. The Euro being the ultimate stupidity. But they will reach an arrangement on tariifs etc and wiser head will prevail with regards to the transatlantic alliance. 

Meanwhile  in the Anglosphere talks are already underway about CANZUK an alliance with free movement of people and goods between Canada, New Zealand UK and Australia. They already have the same head of state ( God save the king) Such a collection would be around 6.5% world GDP and have a surface area larger than Russia and a population of similar size to Russia.

Already Trump is getting sicken by Putin as has been heard to say this weekend.

## **SPX 0DTE Road Map – Monday, March 31**  
**Context:**  
- Largest short straddle in recent memory (~20k) sits directly under SPX  
- VIX1D > VIX9D < VIX > VIX3M → compression possible, but only *after* the explosion  
- VVIX > 100 = volatile volatility  
- **Dealer flows extremely reactive** today — expect rips/dips, not trends  
- **JPM 5565 = Gamma Apex / Unwind Trigger**

---

### **Range Logic Zones & IF/THEN Scenarios**

---

### 🔸 **Within Gamma Trough: 5480–5565**

- **IF price holds 5500–5565**  
  → Stop-hunt territory  
  → Dealer hedging whipsaws flow  
  → Pure chop zone  
  → **Scalp only / no trend trades**  
  → Vol stays bid unless escape occurs  
  → Flip watch: **5565 (upside)** / **5480 (downside)**

---

### 🔼 **Above Gamma Trough: 5565+**

- **IF > 5565 (JPM Put Strike)**  
  → Gamma unwind in play  
  → Rapid shift to long gamma hedging  
  → **Target: 5600 → 5625 stall**  
  → **IF vol drops / VIX compresses** → push into **5650**  
  → **IF > 5625/30 late-day** → squeeze fades, dealer flows neutralize

---

### 🔽 **Below Gamma Trough: < 5480**

- **IF < 5480**  
  → Dealer delta flips hard short  
  → Flush to **5450 magnet**  
  → **IF < 5450** → slide risk to **5400/5375 zone**  
  → No support until panic flows exhaust or vol crush hits  

---

### **Volatility Structure Implications (Summary)**

- **VIX1D: 25.4 > VIX9D** → Front-loaded risk  
- **VVIX > 100** → Expect acceleration on failed supports/resistances  
- Compression plays (calendars/diagonals) only valid *after* move escapes trough

---

comment.body:
Here’s a plain-language breakdown of Alma from GPT
Plain-Language Breakdown – SPX Directional Read
🔹 Main Pivot (Volatility-Based): ~5610
This is the upper pivot based on the vol surface.
IF bulls break above ~5590, then 5610 is the next key.
A sustained move above 5610 likely means the squeeze is active and stabilizing.
🔻 Bear Pivot: ~5565 (JPM Put Strike)
This is the major gamma hinge — if price stays below it, hedging pressure supports downside.
It's also the short strike from the JPM collar — meaning vol stays bid below here.
🔸 Neutral Zone (Vanna Flatline): ~5585
At this level, vanna flips from negative to zero.
IF price is stuck around 5585, movement slows unless vol drops sharply.
It’s like the “neutral buoyancy” line for directional flows.
🔻 Support Zone: ~5520, with Flow Drag to ~5490
Around 5520, longer-dated put gamma starts stabilizing price.
If price drops here with vol staying high, that’s normal dealer behavior (absorbing flow).
~5490 is the drag zone — could get pulled there mechanically.
🔼 Bull Target with Confirmation: ~5660
If price clears 5610 and vol drops, bulls need ~5660 to break and stabilize an upward path.
This level would indicate the end of dealer resistance and start of “clean air.”
Charm Flip Zone: ~5575
Below this = charm is positive (price wants to drift up with time decay)
Above this = charm turns negative (decay pulls price lower)
Today, that means gamma decay throughout the session increases volatility, especially if we stay above 5515.
🧠 Additional Insight (from Vanna Commentary):
Below 5565:
MM deltas make higher lows on vol up, lower highs on vol down
This is a natural vanna compression zone = "boxy" price action unless external flows intervene.
IF slope flattens → normal flow
IF slope steepens (~5%) → signals foreign or institutional directional pressure
✅ Summary of SPX Directional Map (Rounded)
ZoneAction / Read5660+Squeeze confirmed, stabilize uptrend on vol crush5610Main pivot – needs bull momentum + vol drop to flip5585Neutral gamma/vanna – stall zone, slow unless vol shifts5575Charm flip – decay increases above here = more volatility5565Dealer short gamma strike (JPM) – below = downside flows kick in5520Longer-dated gamma starts absorbing – stabilization support5490Natural flow drag if downside accelerates

Although I expected an intraday bull trap late last week, it didn’t happen. However, this momentum still suggests to me that we might see lower realized volatility and a higher spot before a vol event occurs. 
Some funds are aiming for buying flow on Monday, while market makers’ net delta exposure is positive for Monday. 
5560.92 – 5532.86 is a key zone to watch for the week (with ultimate pivot of 5504.85) if downside vol materializes by Friday. If we hold above this line with relatively lower fixed-strike vol and RV, we can still expect a bull trap into Friday.
I’m personally not trading this setup. I’m waiting patiently. If I see the sign, I might take a small allocation in a short-term long delta, short vol trade. But if the bounce happens, I’ll use it as an opportunity to add to my long vol, short delta position - as I keep saying.
You don’t have to trade every wave. The end is near. And hidden risk will be flushed out soon

comment.body:
Here’s a plain-language breakdown of Alma from GPT
Plain-Language Breakdown – SPX Directional Read
🔹 Main Pivot (Volatility-Based): ~5610
This is the upper pivot based on the vol surface.
IF bulls break above ~5590, then 5610 is the next key.
A sustained move above 5610 likely means the squeeze is active and stabilizing.
🔻 Bear Pivot: ~5565 (JPM Put Strike)
This is the major gamma hinge — if price stays below it, hedging pressure supports downside.
It's also the short strike from the JPM collar — meaning vol stays bid below here.
🔸 Neutral Zone (Vanna Flatline): ~5585
At this level, vanna flips from negative to zero.
IF price is stuck around 5585, movement slows unless vol drops sharply.
It’s like the “neutral buoyancy” line for directional flows.
🔻 Support Zone: ~5520, with Flow Drag to ~5490
Around 5520, longer-dated put gamma starts stabilizing price.
If price drops here with vol staying high, that’s normal dealer behavior (absorbing flow).
~5490 is the drag zone — could get pulled there mechanically.
🔼 Bull Target with Confirmation: ~5660
If price clears 5610 and vol drops, bulls need ~5660 to break and stabilize an upward path.
This level would indicate the end of dealer resistance and start of “clean air.”
Charm Flip Zone: ~5575
Below this = charm is positive (price wants to drift up with time decay)
Above this = charm turns negative (decay pulls price lower)
Today, that means gamma decay throughout the session increases volatility, especially if we stay above 5515.
🧠 Additional Insight (from Vanna Commentary):
Below 5565:
MM deltas make higher lows on vol up, lower highs on vol down
This is a natural vanna compression zone = "boxy" price action unless external flows intervene.
IF slope flattens → normal flow
IF slope steepens (~5%) → signals foreign or institutional directional pressure
✅ Summary of SPX Directional Map (Rounded)
ZoneAction / Read5660+Squeeze confirmed, stabilize uptrend on vol crush5610Main pivot – needs bull momentum + vol drop to flip5585Neutral gamma/vanna – stall zone, slow unless vol shifts5575Charm flip – decay increases above here = more volatility5565Dealer short gamma strike (JPM) – below = downside flows kick in5520Longer-dated gamma starts absorbing – stabilization support5490Natural flow drag if downside accelerates

comment.body:
You need to read Alma's posts. She has detailed out the collar extensively in many posts.  Get your money's worth.  

From this morning...."If the spot is above the long put strike (5565), there can be only a mild supportive effect. The OTM expiring put spread provides minor support, which the new collar largely absorbs. However, if the put spread expires ITM, then short vanna, long charm, and short speed create a suppressive environment toward the lower leg (4700), roughly towards 5114.24
The collar’s psychological impact is bigger than its actual influence on flows. And the funds know this, which is why they play with it."

Guys, the key is:
Vol events NEVER ... i repeat: NEVER occur when market is hedged. They occur when market is complacent about something and it misprices the risk. 
Like in last July. Spot up/vol up, tech squeeze, while I kept posting on twitter about that the Fed wants to cut rates in sept, but they need room for that. They must cut bcs of election, and to boost the growth in election year. But for that they need room. No rate cut at ATHs.
Then boom... market fell like no tomorrow

Market i misinterprets the risk of the tariffs. It is complacent. Perfect bulltrap. Even SPX/LTY correlation is positive. 
Only that CDS is telling us that it doesn't resonates well with each other... something is off. and you have to understand the story.

comment.body:
i tell you something...
big investments funds and hedge funds, quant trading firms etc. don't fully rely on market datas, positionings, vol metrics, momentum metrics, etc.
they have deep learning algos to monitoring sentiment via soc medias, news, economic datas, politics datas... even weather.. and some of them use religious fests and astrology too, in order to get a good pic (these last two things may seem stupid, but it is not, if i tell you WHY they are doing it... it is cryptographic btw)

and while the market is roughly efficient most of the times, the tail events occur when it is not.

Now, the problem is that as the world order is changing, there is a lot of random factors that cannot be predicted.
I.e. a successfull assasination of Trump.. or China invades Taiwan... or EU invades Ukraine... or a certain economic participant refuses a deal with another one etc.

These are random factors, and mostly irrational. You can't predict them. Hence lower liquidity, higher sticky vol.
Why? bcs the world order is changing, so the market simply CANNOT BE efficient

this is crucial. And this is why funds are monitoring the other things that are happening arond the world, to have a benchmark... to gain probability distributions on those events too.
And using these outputs, you can compare the market pricing of those events, and estimate a rate of the market efficiency or inefficiency.

clear?

comment.body: This may be what some folks are getting at by saying the market is unhedged or underhedged.  This is from OD and is customer delta exposure for the month of April (except the top left panel is delta trend since mid-March, which shows customer delta exposure for puts recently trending from long (negative delta) to short (positive delta)).  As I read this looking forward, delta exposures by date (bottom left panel), we see very little negative delta -- the green bars pointing down are short calls, red bars down are long puts, and vice versa going up.  Many more short puts than long puts, by delta exposure.  Looking at the right panel (customer delta exposure by strike for April), there's long put exposure at 5500 (and at 5600) but not much else below.  As always, I welcome comments and correction if I am missing something or not reading correctly.

comment.body:
Yes I am well away that everything from flying in a plane, eating from a seedy Chinese restaurant, or trading the markets is a probability distribution. Everything in life is. That said, because a plane could crash, does that mean I should refuse to do fly? No.

So in a service where we are looking to gain an edge by increasing our understanding of a probability distribution, how is it helpful or insightful to say “the market could go up or sideways over the next 2 days to 3 months as we set up a “bull trap”? Especially when the commentary of that probability distribution doesn’t look further out than 2-3 months? It’s actually like saying “the market could go sideways, raging up by 200-300 points, or crash 200-500 points over the next 2 months.”

I don’t know why this is hard for everyone to comprehend here. It’s like this thread has an autoimmune response to false pathogens.

comment.body:
Respectfully, I think you're focused on one part (the PA bull trap), then asking and looking only for specifics about that one part, not the entirety of what is unfolding. I get it though. The exact area of PA would be nice to know when to short or go long, but we all know nobody knows how the market will unfold day by day. 

Alma has been giving the macro context (as well as the quant details), geo and market dynamics, to show us how the market is currently mispricing a risk event. And if you recall in her mean reversion post, she refers to periods of time where that mispricing leads to corrections. The momentum pendulum swings and the psychology of that moment (which is apart of a trap, bear or bull) creates a volatility event. And so at the current time with less risk being priced in, if that momentum builds quickly, and the PA floats upward, thats when the swift move down can happen. 

That's how I am interpreting the idea and point of a bull trap.

My 2 cents. The consistent thing that you can be sure is actionable that you will get from this service are the intraday levels and the dealer flow analysis.

Anything beyond a day but probably a good read on the week are her weekly pivots and then goes to longer positioning like JPM collar or iron condor man. 

And then outside of this - what you’re getting is interpreting of the fed actions, the geo politician actions, and possibly quarter end options expiry/vixperation analysis. 

It’s like the layers to the onion. There are some core things that are very much actionable. But the speculative outer layers you have to understand are stabs at predictions the future that Alma is making her best interpretation of 
for our benefit. I’d rather have those interpretations than not have them because some educated guess is better than a zero guess from me

comment.body: A comment from the sidelines on potential timing.  As I understand Alma's weekly post (and not trying to put any words in her mouth), she suggests that because in relative terms skew is not bid, the market is underpricing a potential [insert your favorite word -- I'll use sh!tstorm] from the impending tariff issues, both whatever Trump does and however the EU might respond, with an emphasis on under appreciation of how strong the EU's response may be.  In making this observation, she looks at the skew for next week, April 15, and April OpEx.  My inference therefore is that the timeline for such an event -- and the timing of the bull trap setup -- is within 1-3 weeks, not a setup that might take months and months to play out.  This fits in with her broader view of timing that she's referenced repeatedly of a bottoming [note the use of the gerund, not a "bottom" but a "bottoming," i.e., a process] after Easter and into June.  And accepting that the goal of this bottoming is to provide a stable foundation for growth, it seems unlikely to be V-bottom, followed by lockout rally (think Nov 2023).  Putting this together in my mind, a potential downside surprise over the coming weeks precipitating a big drop, which then takes all wind out of the market's sails, the market then churns and grinds lower in a bottoming process through May into June as the geopolitical events Alma has described unfold and potentially resolve.

Guys, I’m glad there’s a discussion going on about what I wrote. The problem is that, first, some people are taking one sentence out of context and trying to interpret everything rigidly. Second, I see some trying to start from the wrong premises, and apparently they haven’t read my writings in their entirety.
A bull trap aims to lure traders and investors expecting prices to rise into a bad trade on both the spot and the time axes.
It’s exactly that situation where the trade seems too good to be true.
By itself, whether a price moves up or down says nothing. The volatility assigned to it, however, does, as does its interaction (correlation) with other assets.
In the current economic structure, there’s a “conventional” or “healthy” dynamic to these. If the dynamic goes off-kilter while the price, for example, shows a bullish trend, then you know something’s off. This might be a spot up / VIX up scenario, or gold, equities, bonds, oil, and JPY all moving in the same direction, etc., while the price might have already blown through three-sigma events… in such cases, you know one of those trends is lying, and it’s up to the analyst to figure out which one.

comment.body:
Here is my understanding of it: 
1.) "need to see lower intraday vol to break JPM strike": Vanna is 0 for ATM options so if we're at 5565 and volatility increases then its delta will start to get more negative pushing us back down. If it decreases then its delta starts to go down which makes it supportive.
2.) MM Long delta/vol exposure: Above the strike MM will get longer deltas and longer volatility (so you can think of it as they're being sold calls). Because its a positive speed environment the market is likely to start moving up but its movement is more a natural consequence of the underlying options positioning so don't read into it too much. Now if the rate it moves shows any sort of divergence (like lets say speed profile changes a lot) then we might be able to figure out what the real move is.

comment.body:
i tell you something...
big investments funds and hedge funds, quant trading firms etc. don't fully rely on market datas, positionings, vol metrics, momentum metrics, etc.
they have deep learning algos to monitoring sentiment via soc medias, news, economic datas, politics datas... even weather.. and some of them use religious fests and astrology too, in order to get a good pic (these last two things may seem stupid, but it is not, if i tell you WHY they are doing it... it is cryptographic btw)

and while the market is roughly efficient most of the times, the tail events occur when it is not.

Now, the problem is that as the world order is changing, there is a lot of random factors that cannot be predicted.
I.e. a successfull assasination of Trump.. or China invades Taiwan... or EU invades Ukraine... or a certain economic participant refuses a deal with another one etc.

These are random factors, and mostly irrational. You can't predict them. Hence lower liquidity, higher sticky vol.
Why? bcs the world order is changing, so the market simply CANNOT BE efficient

this is crucial. And this is why funds are monitoring the other things that are happening arond the world, to have a benchmark... to gain probability distributions on those events too.
And using these outputs, you can compare the market pricing of those events, and estimate a rate of the market efficiency or inefficiency.

clear?

All I simply said is when you say “bull traps are a setup”, it seems you are giving a lot of cushion for this to occur on the “time axis”. If there is too much cushion on that axis, as you just said, then it can lead to a bad trade. I gave the example of price surging to 5900 as a hypothetical example that would still fit into that broad statement, and the very broad statement that you “hearted” in response to it, which stated “bull trap” setups can “take days, weeks, or months”. As I mentioned, if this setup did take weeks or months, then that would go against your weekend analysis, which I read. My whole point of saying this is those statements can lead to a plethora of potential outcomes. Stating this seems to be unhelpful and not practical. It also undermines and seems to give “an out” if your “left tail risk event” takes longer to manifest than anticipated.

Also, gold is surging to new all time highs while SPX is correcting. They are not moving in unison. Also, VIX has not been moving up with price, as I’ve pointed out many times. Volatility came down on the last “bull trap” when we almost tagged 5800. VIX and volatility have come up with price both times it pierced below the 5565 JPM strike and never stayed down for more than half of a trading day. Then VIX has come down as price has gone back above 5565.

Also, quick question, what was off with the “conventional economic structure” of the “bull trap” that occurred after Trump’s election win?  Would be interesting to know.

Also, I think many people are concerned about potential recession risks. Does this AAII survey look like it’s not expecting further bearish action to you?
